# Feature: Wave Schema Versioning (Collection & Environment)

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-001
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_multi_component_fixes.md`

Give the two persisted Wave file formats ‚Äî collections and environments ‚Äî a formal, documented, versioned schema (v0.0.1) backed by Zod validation at the import and load boundaries. Users get clear, actionable errors when importing malformed files instead of silent corruption or cryptic crashes; the team gets a single canonical schema reference (`docs/schemas.md`) and a single source of truth for the version constant (today `'0.0.1'` is scattered as a literal across `BaseCollectionTransformer`, `CollectionService`, and a dozen test fixtures). This feature is the foundation for FEAT-002 (`.http` transformer), FEAT-005 (content detection), and FEAT-006 (environment import), all of which stamp or validate against these schemas.

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

> No `.spec-lite/data_model.md` exists ‚Äî the granular model is designed here from the plan's conceptual model and the existing TypeScript types in `packages/core/src/types/collection.ts`. The Zod schemas formalize the *already-persisted* shape; this feature changes the persisted data only by (a) making `info.version` consistently stamped and (b) adding `Environment.version`.

### Wave Collection Schema v0.0.1

Mirrors the existing `Collection` interface ([collection.ts:319](../../packages/core/src/types/collection.ts)):

- **Collection**:
  - `info` (CollectionInfo) ‚Äî required
  - `item` (CollectionItem[]) ‚Äî required (may be empty)
  - `filename` (string, optional) ‚Äî runtime-only, added at load; **excluded from the persisted schema** (validation must not require it, and it is tolerated if present)
- **CollectionInfo**:
  - `waveId` (string) ‚Äî stable logical identity; required in schema; loaders stamp a generated id when absent in legacy files *before* validation
  - `name` (string) ‚Äî required, non-empty
  - `description` (string, optional)
  - `schema` (string, optional) ‚Äî legacy/reserved field, kept
  - `version` (string) ‚Äî schema version; required in schema; loaders/importers stamp `CURRENT_COLLECTION_SCHEMA_VERSION` (`'0.0.1'`) when absent before validation
- **CollectionItem** (recursive via `z.lazy`):
  - `id` (string) ‚Äî required (loaders already stamp via `ensureCollectionItemIds`)
  - `name` (string) ‚Äî required
  - `description` (string, optional)
  - `request` (AnyCollectionRequest, optional)
  - `response` (array, optional) ‚Äî validated permissively as `z.array(z.unknown())` in v0.0.1 (opaque; tightening is a future schema version)
  - `item` (CollectionItem[], optional) ‚Äî folder children, arbitrary nesting
- **AnyCollectionRequest** ‚Äî union of three request shapes discriminated by `protocol`:
  - HTTP (`protocol` absent or `'http'`): `id`, `name`, `method` required; `url` (string | CollectionUrl), `query?` (ParamRow[]), `header?` (HeaderRow[]), `body?` (CollectionBody), `description?`, `validation?` (opaque `z.unknown()` in v0.0.1), `authId?`, `sourceRef?`
  - WS (`protocol: 'ws'`): as above minus `method`/`body`/`validation`
  - SSE (`protocol: 'sse'`): as HTTP minus `validation`
- **CollectionBody** ‚Äî discriminated union on `mode`: `none` | `raw` (raw: string, options?) | `urlencoded` (FormField[]) | `formdata` (MultiPartFormField[]) | `file` (file?: FileReference)
- **Row/field shapes**: `ParamRow`/`HeaderRow` (`id`, `key`, `value`, `disabled`), `FormField` (`value: string | null`), `MultiPartFormField` (`value: string | FileReference | null`, `fieldType: 'text' | 'file'`), `FileReference` (path, fileName, contentType, size, pathType, storageType, fileData?), `CollectionUrl` (`raw` required; protocol/host/path/query optional), `CollectionReference` (collectionFilename, collectionName, itemPath: string[])

### Wave Environment Schema v0.0.1

- **Environment** ([collection.ts:364](../../packages/core/src/types/collection.ts)):
  - `id` (string) ‚Äî required
  - `name` (string) ‚Äî required, non-empty
  - `version` (string) ‚Äî **NEW optional field on the TS type**; required in schema after stamping; loaders/importers stamp `CURRENT_ENVIRONMENT_SCHEMA_VERSION` (`'0.0.1'`) when absent
  - `values` (EnvironmentVariable[]) ‚Äî required (may be empty)
- **EnvironmentVariable**:
  - `key` (string) ‚Äî required
  - `value` (string) ‚Äî required
  - `type` (`'default' | 'secret'`) ‚Äî required
  - `notes` (string, optional)
  - `enabled` (boolean) ‚Äî required

### Relationships

- Collection 1:N CollectionItem (recursive tree via `item[]`); CollectionItem 0..1 AnyCollectionRequest via `request`.
- Environment 1:N EnvironmentVariable via `values[]` (flat list).

### Indexes & Constraints

- File-based JSON storage (unchanged) ‚Äî no DB indexes. `filename` is the on-disk key; `info.waveId` / `Environment.id` are logical identities.
- **Validation semantics**: schemas are *validate-only* ‚Äî `safeParse` checks shape, but on success the helpers return the **original input object** typed as `Collection`/`Environment` (not Zod's parsed copy), so unknown extra fields in user files survive load/save round-trips. v0.0.1 is deliberately tolerant: opaque sub-shapes (`validation`, `response`) use `z.unknown()`, documented as such.
- **Schema version vs. package version**: independent tracks (FEAT-012). A schema version bump requires a migration note in `docs/schemas.md`.

## 3. Files

- `packages/core/package.json` ‚Äî add `zod` dependency (`^3.25`, per memory standard)
- `packages/core/src/schemas/collectionSchema.ts` ‚Äî NEW: `WaveCollectionSchema`, `CURRENT_COLLECTION_SCHEMA_VERSION`, `validateWaveCollection()`
- `packages/core/src/schemas/environmentSchema.ts` ‚Äî NEW: `WaveEnvironmentSchema`, `CURRENT_ENVIRONMENT_SCHEMA_VERSION`, `validateWaveEnvironment()`

- `packages/shared/src/services/EnvironmentService.ts` ‚Äî stamp `version`; validate in `loadAll`, `import`, `save`

- `docs/README.md`, `docs/features/collections.md`, `docs/features/environments.md` ‚Äî link/reference schema docs
- `packages/core/src/test/schemas/collectionSchema.test.ts` ‚Äî NEW: unit tests
- `packages/core/src/test/schemas/environmentSchema.test.ts` ‚Äî NEW: unit tests
- `packages/shared/src/test/services/CollectionService.test.ts` ‚Äî extend: validation-at-boundary cases
- `packages/shared/src/test/services/EnvironmentService.test.ts` ‚Äî extend: version stamping + validation cases

## 4. Dependencies

- None (first feature of the plan, deliberately ordered first).
- Downstream consumers: FEAT-002 (HttpFileTransformer stamps the constant), FEAT-005 (Wave detection can lean on `WaveCollectionSchema`), FEAT-006 (environment import validates with `WaveEnvironmentSchema`).

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Collection Zod schema + version constant in core

- [ ] **Implementation**: Add `zod` (`^3.25`) to `packages/core` dependencies (pnpm workspace; note core is bundled into the web app and vscode webview ‚Äî zod is already the project-standard boundary validator per memory). Create `packages/core/src/schemas/collectionSchema.ts` exporting:
  - `CURRENT_COLLECTION_SCHEMA_VERSION = '0.0.1'` (module-level `const`, UPPER_SNAKE_CASE),
  - `WaveCollectionSchema` built per the data model above ‚Äî `z.lazy` for the recursive `CollectionItem`, `z.discriminatedUnion('mode', ‚Ä¶)` for `CollectionBody`, a `z.union` for `AnyCollectionRequest` (HTTP variant accepts absent `protocol`), `z.unknown()` for opaque `validation`/`response` fields, and `filename` tolerated as optional,
  - `validateWaveCollection(data: unknown): Result<Collection, string>` ‚Äî `safeParse`; on success return `ok(data as Collection)` (validate-only, preserves unknown fields); on failure return `err(...)` with a readable message listing up to the first 5 issues as `path: message` joined with `'; '`.
  Create `packages/core/src/schemas/index.ts` barrel and export from `packages/core/src/index.ts`. JSDoc on every export.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `packages/core/src/test/schemas/collectionSchema.test.ts` ‚Äî valid minimal collection passes; valid deep collection (nested folders, http+ws+sse requests, all five body modes, `CollectionUrl` object form, string url form) passes; legacy http request without `protocol` passes; missing `info.name` fails with path in message; missing `info.version` fails (stamping happens upstream); item missing `id` fails; wrong `body.mode` payload fails; unknown extra top-level field passes and is preserved in the returned value (`ok` value `===` input); non-object input (`null`, string, array) fails gracefully.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc with `@example` on `validateWaveCollection`; inline comment explaining the validate-only (no-strip) decision.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- schemas` green; `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core typecheck` and `build` pass (zod bundles cleanly in the Vite library build).

### TASK-002: Environment Zod schema + `Environment.version` field

- [ ] **Implementation**: Add `version?: string` to `Environment` in `packages/core/src/types/collection.ts` (with JSDoc tying it to the schema version, not package versions). Create `packages/core/src/schemas/environmentSchema.ts` exporting `CURRENT_ENVIRONMENT_SCHEMA_VERSION = '0.0.1'`, `WaveEnvironmentSchema` (per data model; `version` required by the schema ‚Äî callers stamp before validating), and `validateWaveEnvironment(data: unknown): Result<Environment, string>` (same validate-only/error-formatting conventions as TASK-001). Export via the schemas barrel.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `packages/core/src/test/schemas/environmentSchema.test.ts` ‚Äî valid env passes; empty `values` passes; variable with `type: 'secret'` and `notes` passes; missing `version` fails; invalid `type` value fails; missing `enabled` fails; non-object input fails gracefully; extra unknown fields preserved.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the new `version` field and schema exports.
- **Verify**: core tests + typecheck green; `pnpm typecheck` at root confirms no downstream breakage from the optional field.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001 (shares the `schemas/` module and conventions)

### TASK-003: Consolidate the scattered `'0.0.1'` version literals

- [ ] **Implementation**: Delete `protected readonly waveVersion = '0.0.1'` from `BaseCollectionTransformer` ([BaseCollectionTransformer.ts:36](../../packages/core/src/utils/transformers/BaseCollectionTransformer.ts)) and update the four transformers (`WaveCollectionTransformer`, `PostmanCollectionTransformer`, `HttpFileTransformer`, `SwaggerTransformer`) to stamp `info.version` from `CURRENT_COLLECTION_SCHEMA_VERSION` (import from `../../schemas`). Delete `private readonly waveVersion = '0.0.1'` from `CollectionService` ([CollectionService.ts:189](../../packages/shared/src/services/CollectionService.ts)) and import the constant from `@wave-client/core` at its three use sites. Per memory, no backwards-compat shims ‚Äî remove the fields cleanly.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Existing transformer tests (`WaveCollectionTransformer.test.ts`, `PostmanCollectionTransformer.test.ts`, `SwaggerTransformer.test.ts`) and `CollectionService.test.ts` already assert `version: '0.0.1'` ‚Äî they must stay green unchanged (regression guard). Add one assertion per transformer that `info.version === CURRENT_COLLECTION_SCHEMA_VERSION` (constant, not literal) so a future bump can't silently diverge.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: None beyond JSDoc (covered in docs task).
- **Verify**: `Grep` for `waveVersion` returns no hits in `packages/core` and `packages/shared` source; `pnpm test` (core + shared) green.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

### TASK-004: Validate collections at load & import boundaries

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `CollectionService`:
  - `loadOne`: keep the existing normalization order (ensure item ids, stamp `waveId`, stamp `info.version` from the constant), **then** run `validateWaveCollection`. On failure: log an error with filename, waveId, operation (`loadOne`), and the validation message (per plan logging strategy ‚Äî these failures were silent before), and return `null` (preserves the existing contract: `loadAll` skips bad files and the rest of the pane still loads).
  - `saveFromContent` (the import boundary ‚Äî reached from vscode `MessageHandler.handleImportCollection` and the server collections route): wrap `JSON.parse` to produce a descriptive error (`Invalid JSON: ‚Ä¶`); normalize (ids, waveId, version stamp) then validate; on failure throw `Error(validationMessage)` instead of persisting. The thrown error follows the existing service‚Üíhandler contract: MessageHandler / the Express route catch it and return an error response, which the adapters surface as `Result.err` ‚Üí UI notification (satisfying the plan's "Result errors, not throws" at the boundary users see).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Extend `packages/shared/src/test/services/CollectionService.test.ts` ‚Äî `loadOne` returns `null` and logs for a structurally invalid file (e.g., `item` not an array); `loadOne` stamps `version`/`waveId` on a legacy file then passes validation; `saveFromContent` rejects invalid JSON with a descriptive message; `saveFromContent` rejects shape-invalid collection and does **not** write the file; `saveFromContent` accepts and stamps a valid collection missing `version`.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc updates on `loadOne`/`saveFromContent` describing validation behavior.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/shared test` green; manual check ‚Äî import a malformed collection JSON via the Collections pane import wizard and confirm a readable error notification appears and no file is created in the collections dir.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001, TASK-003

### TASK-005: Validate environments at load & import boundaries + version stamping

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `EnvironmentService`:
  - `loadAll`: after reading each file, stamp `version` with `CURRENT_ENVIRONMENT_SCHEMA_VERSION` when absent (mirrors `CollectionService.loadOne` stamping), then `validateWaveEnvironment`; on failure log (filename, env id/name, operation) and skip that file (existing per-file try/catch continues the loop). Stamp `version` on the default Global environment too.
  - `save`: stamp `version` when absent before writing (so every persisted env carries the version going forward).
  - `import`: wrap `JSON.parse` with a descriptive error; validate **each** environment (after version stamping) before saving any ‚Äî all-or-nothing: if one entry is invalid, throw with a message identifying which entry (by index/name) failed, and persist none.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Extend/create `packages/shared/src/test/services/EnvironmentService.test.ts` ‚Äî `loadAll` stamps `version` on a legacy versionless file; `loadAll` skips an invalid file but still returns valid ones plus Global; `save` writes `version` into the JSON; `import` of a valid array persists all with versions; `import` with one invalid entry persists nothing and names the offender; `import` of invalid JSON throws a descriptive error.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc updates on the changed methods.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/shared test` green; manual check ‚Äî import a malformed environment JSON via the Environments pane and confirm a readable error notification; open an existing environments JSON file on disk after an edit-and-save and confirm `"version": "0.0.1"` is present.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002

### TASK-006: Canonical schema documentation (`docs/schemas.md`)

- [ ] **Implementation**: Create `docs/schemas.md` ‚Äî the canonical schema reference going forward ‚Äî containing: (1) purpose and the schema-version-vs-package-version distinction (cross-reference the FEAT-012 versioning doc as a forward pointer); (2) **Wave Collection Schema v0.0.1** ‚Äî every field from the data model above with type, required/optional, and semantics, including the opaque-in-v0.0.1 fields (`validation`, `response`) and the runtime-only `filename`; (3) **Wave Environment Schema v0.0.1** ‚Äî same treatment; (4) a **Version History** section starting at `0.0.1 ‚Äî initial formalized schema` with the rule that any persisted-shape change bumps the version and adds a migration note here; (5) a short "where validation happens" section (import + load boundaries). Link it from `docs/README.md` and reference it from `docs/features/collections.md` and `docs/features/environments.md`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: N/A (documentation) ‚Äî per the testing convention, no tests for docs.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: This task *is* the documentation; also verify JSDoc from TASK-001‚Äì005 is consistent with it.
- **Verify**: Manual review ‚Äî every field in `WaveCollectionSchema`/`WaveEnvironmentSchema` appears in `docs/schemas.md`; links from `docs/README.md` and the two feature docs resolve.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001, TASK-002

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: Validation helpers in core return `Result<T, string>` (never throw), per the Result pattern in memory. Services translate the error branch into their existing channel contracts: `loadOne`/`loadAll` log-and-skip (UI keeps working with the valid files), import paths throw descriptive `Error`s that MessageHandler / server routes convert into error responses, which adapters surface as `Result.err` and the UI shows via `notification.showNotification('error', ‚Ä¶)`. No new error channels.
- **Logging**: Per the plan's logging strategy ‚Äî every validation failure in `CollectionService`/`EnvironmentService` logs with context (filename, waveId / env id, operation, validation message); these were silent failure paths before. Never log file contents or variable values (environment values may contain secrets).
- **Security**: This feature implements the memory rule "validate all external input with Zod" at exactly the untrusted-input boundaries (imported and on-disk JSON files).

- [x] TASK-005: Validate environments at load & import boundaries + version stamping

> **Implementation notes (2026-06-10)**: Row-level `id`/`disabled` made optional in the schema because the app's own save path (`sanitizeRequestForSave`) persists minimal rows. Load normalization extended to stamp missing `request.id`/`request.name` (from the wrapping item) and to default environment variable `type`/`enabled` ‚Äî legacy files pass validation per the stamp-then-validate rule. `CollectionService.import` validates in addition to `saveFromContent` (both are import boundaries).
